title: mfa — notification classes + categories
description: Five notifications. Two are informational-transactional (MFA-enrolled, MFA-disabled — non-actionable, informing the user their MFA posture changed); one is high-priority security (recovery-code consumed — signals possible account compromise); one is a periodic reminder (low recovery codes); one is a security incident alert (suspicious activity — repeated failed challenges from novel IPs). All render through the localized template pipeline — subject + body support {{placeholders}} resolved at dispatch time.

notification_definitions.default_subject_en: Two-factor authentication is now active on your Academorix account
notification_definitions.notes: Non-actionable confirmation. Body highlights recovery-code storage — first-time enrolments especially benefit from a nudge to save the codes offline. On WebAuthn credential registration (not first enrolment) the copy is different — a shorter 'new hardware key registered' body.

notification_definitions.default_subject_en: Two-factor authentication was disabled on your Academorix account

payload_bindings.actor_label: 'You' | 'Academorix support' | 'The system, as part of account erasure'

notification_definitions.notes: Fires on the cumulative MFA-disabled event. Escalation-worthy — if the user did not initiate this, they should reset their password immediately (link included in the body). Non-optional for compliance.

notification_definitions.default_subject_en: A recovery code was used to sign in to your Academorix account
notification_definitions.notes: High-signal security notification. Recovery codes are the fallback path — a legitimate user consuming one means their primary MFA device is unavailable (lost / broken / left at home); an attacker consuming one means they had prior access to the codes. Body prompts the user to (a) regenerate remaining codes, (b) reset their password if the request is unfamiliar.

notification_definitions.default_subject_en: You're running low on Academorix recovery codes
notification_definitions.notes: Non-actionable-but-recommended. Debounced 30d per Identity — a user who ignores the first reminder gets a second one after 30 days, not every night. Persists as an in-app dot on /me/mfa until the user regenerates OR dismisses.

notification_definitions.default_subject_en: Unusual two-factor authentication activity on your Academorix account
notification_definitions.notes: Dispatched by the SuspiciousActivityDetector when the aggregated signal crosses threshold: 5+ MfaChallengeFailed events for the same Identity within 15 minutes across 2+ distinct IPs. Body walks the user through immediate remediation: revoke sessions + reset password + regenerate recovery codes. Also dispatched to the platform-security-alerts channel with more detail (redacted for GDPR — no IPs geolocated to precise addresses, just city + country).

categories.description: Confirms your account now requires a second factor.
Confirms your account's second-factor protection was removed.
Alerts you every time a recovery code is used to sign in. Non-optional so account compromise is always visible.
Reminds you to regenerate recovery codes when only a few remain.
High-frequency + novel-IP MFA failure signal. Non-optional so account compromise is always escalated.
